content:
As of my knowledge cutoff in June 2024, there has been no official update to the Federal Rules of Evidence or patent litigation standards explicitly introducing a "revised Daubert-Frye convergence standard" with a reproducibility index (RI) threshold of 0.7 for admitting expert testimony in patent cases. The Daubert standard (from Daubert v. Merrell Dow Pharmaceuticals) and the Frye standard (from Frye v. United States) remain distinct approaches to assessing expert testimony admissibility, and no formal convergence or numeric reproducibility index requirement has been codified in federal rules.

If you are referring to a hypothetical or proposed standard, or a specific jurisdiction or court pilot program, then the concept of a reproducibility index (RI) would be a novel quantitative metric intended to measure how reliably an expert's methodology can be replicated by others.
